94th }} The '''93rd Academy Awards''' ceremony, presented by the [[Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences]] (AMPAS), honored films released from January 1, 2020, to February 28, 2021, at [[Union Station (Los Angeles)|Union Station]] in [[Los Angeles]].{{efn|name=Oscar}} The ceremony was held on April 25, 2021, rather than its usual late-February date due to the [[COVID-19 pandemic]].{{cite news |title=Academy Delays 2021 Oscars Ceremony Because of Coronavirus |url=https://www.tampabay.com/news/nation-world/2020/06/15/academy-delays-2021-oscars-ceremony-because-of-coronavirus/ |access-date=August 12, 2020 |work=[[Tampa Bay Times]] |date=June 15, 2020 |archive-date=June 17, 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200617160434/https://www.tampabay.com/news/nation-world/2020/06/15/academy-delays-2021-oscars-ceremony-because-of-coronavirus/ |url-status=live}} During the ceremony, the AMPAS presented [[Academy Awards]] (commonly referred to as Oscars) in 23 categories. The ceremony, televised in the United States by [[American Broadcasting Company|ABC]], was produced by Jesse Collins, [[Stacey Sher]], and [[Steven Soderbergh]], and was directed by [[Glenn Weiss]].{{cite news |last1=Truitt |first1=Brian |title=Director Steven Soderbergh Will Co-produce upcoming Academy Awards Ceremony film)|The Father]]'', ''[[Judas and the Black Messiah]]'', ''[[Ma Rainey's Black Bottom (film)|Ma Rainey's Black Bottom]]'', ''[[Mank]]'', ''[[Soul (2020 film)|Soul]]'' and ''[[Sound of Metal]]'' with The telecast received mostly negative reviews, and it garnered 10.4 million viewers, making it the least-watched Oscar broadcast since viewership records began [[46th Academy Awards|for the 46th ceremony in 1974]].{{cite news |last1=Lang |first1=Brett |last2=Setodeh |first2=Ramin |last3=Riley |first3=Jenelle |last4=Gleiberman |first4=Owen |last5=Debruge |first5=Peter |title=After Oscars Ratings Tank, Do the Academy Awards Need Another Makeover? |url=https://variety.com/2021/awards/news/oscars-2021-academy-awards-reviews-ratings-1234962181/ |access-date=June 19, 2021 |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=April 28, 2021 |archive-date=May 3, 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210503143925/https://variety.com/2021/awards/news/oscars-2021-academy-awards-reviews-ratings-1234962181/ |url-status=live}}{{cite news |title=Oscars 2021: Audiences Turn Away As a Sluggish Ceremony Leaves Critics Cold |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/entertainment-arts-56885646 |access-date=July 13, 2021 |agency=[[BBC News]] |date=April 26, 2021 |archive-date=August 4, 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210804113257/https://www.bbc.com/news/entertainment-arts-56885646 |url-status=live}} == Winners and nominees == The nominees for the 93rd Academy Awards were announced on March 15, 2021, by actress [[Priyanka Chopra]] and singer [[Nick Jonas]] during a [[Streaming media|live global stream]] originating from [[London]].{{cite news |last1=Siegel |first1=Tatiana |last2=Lewis |first2=Hilary |last3=Konerman |first3=Jennifer |last4=Nordyke |first4=Kimberly |title=Oscars: 'Mank' Leads Nominees With 10 |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/oscars-list-of-nominees-updating-live-4150022/ |access-date=March 19, 2021 |work=[[The Hollywood Reporter]] |date=March 15, 2021 |archive-date=May 5, 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210505231429/https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/oscars-list-of-nominees-updating-live-4150022/ |url-status=live}} ''[[Mank]]'' led all nominees with ten nominations. The winners were announced during the awards ceremony on April 25.{{cite news |title='Nomadland' Wins Best Picture, and Other Highlights from the 2021 Academy Awards |url=https://www.tampabay.com/life-culture/arts/movies/2021/04/25/oscars-updates-highlights-from-the-2021-academy-awards/ |access-date=April 30, 2021 |work=[[Tampa Bay Times]] |date=April 25, 2021 |archive-date=April 29, 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210429095706/https://www.tampabay.com/life-culture/arts/movies/2021/04/25/oscars-updates-highlights-from-the-2021-academy-awards/ |url-status=live}} Chinese filmmaker [[Chloé Zhao]] became the first [[woman of color]] to win Best Director and the second woman overall after [[Kathryn Bigelow]], who won at the [[82nd Academy Awards|2010 ceremony]] for directing ''[[The Hurt Locker]]''.{{cite news |first1=Brooks |last1=Barnes |first2=Nicole |last2=Sperling |title='Nomadland' Makes History, and Anthony Hopkins, in Upset, Wins Best Actor |date=April 25, 2021 |work=[[The New York Times]] |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2021/04/25/movies/academy-awards-oscars.html |access-date=April 26, 2021 |url-access=limited |archive-date=April 27, 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210427205345/https://www.nytimes.com/2021/04/25/movies/academy-awards-oscars.html |url-status=live}} In addition, the nomination for [[Emerald Fennell]] in the same category meant that this also marked the first time two women directors had both been nominated for the Oscar for Best Director in the same year.{{cite web |last1=Aurthur |first1=Kate |title=Oscars Make History by Nominating Two Women Directors |url=https://variety.com/2021/film/news/chloe-zhao-emerald-fennell-best-director-oscar-nominations-1234930889/ |website=Variety |date=March 15, 2021 |access-date=1 July 2025}} At age 83, Best Actor winner [[Anthony Hopkins]] was the oldest performer ever to win a competitive acting Oscar.{{cite news |last=Stevens |first=Matt |date=April 26, 2021 |title=Anthony Hopkins shocks by winning best actor over Chadwick Boseman. |language=en-US |work=[[The New York Times]] |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2021/04/25/movies/anthony-hopkins-chadwick-boseman.html |access-date=April 26, 2021 |issn=0362-4331 |url-access=limited |archive-date=April 26, 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210426041854/https://www.nytimes.com/2021/04/25/movies/anthony-hopkins-chadwick-boseman.html |url-status=live}} Best Actress winner [[Frances McDormand]] became the seventh person to win a third acting Oscar, the third to win three leading performance Oscars, and the second to win Best Actress three times.{{cite news |date=April 25, 2021 |title=Frances McDormand Wins Best Actress: Third Career Oscar, Only Katharine Hepburn Won More in Category |url=https://www.indiewire.com/2021/04/frances-mcdormand-wins-oscar-best-actress-nomadland-1234632159/ ''[[Nomadland]]'', she also was the first person in history to win Oscars for both acting and producing for the same film.{{cite news |url=https://apnews.com/article/2021-oscars-best-actress-frances-mcdormand-569c44257a1592724c67235e7930edc5 |title=Frances McDormand a double Oscar winner for 'Nomadland' |last=Harris |first=Beth Actress winner [[Yuh-jung Youn]] became the first Korean performer and second Asian woman to win an acting Oscar after [[Miyoshi Umeki]], who won the same category for her role in 1957's ''[[Sayonara]]''.{{cite news |last1=Barnes |first1=Brooks |last2=Sperling |first2=Nicole |title='Nomadland' Makes History, and Anthony Hopkins, in Upset, Wins Best Actor |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210427205345/https://www.nytimes.com/2021/04/25/movies/academy-awards-oscars.html |url-status=live}} With his nominations in Best Supporting Actor and Best Original Song for ''[[One Night in Miami...]]'', [[Leslie Odom Jr.]] was the fourth consecutive person to earn acting and songwriting nominations for the same film.{{efn|The three previous individuals to have earned this distinction are [[Mary J. [[File:Los Angeles Union Station Ticket Lobby.jpg|thumb|The original ticket lobby of [[Union Station (Los Angeles)|Los Angeles Union Station]], where the ceremony was held]] In April 2017, the Academy scheduled the 93rd ceremony for February 28, 2021.{{cite news |last1=Hipes |first1=Patrick |title=Oscars Dates Set For 2018 And Beyond |url=https://deadline.com/2017/04/oscars-2018-airdate-abc-academy-awards-dates-1202061171/ |access-date=June 22, 2020 |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |date=April 4, 2017 |archive-date=April 5, 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170405053317/http://deadline.com/2017/04/oscars-2018-airdate-abc-academy-awards-dates-1202061171/ |url-status=live}} However, due to the impacts stemming from the [[COVID-19 pandemic]] on both [[Impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on cinema|cinema]] and [[Impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on television in the United States|television]], the AMPAS Board of Governors later decided to move the date for the 2021 gala by two months to April 25.{{cite news |last1=Malkin |first1=Marc |title=Oscars 2021 Pushed Back by Two Months |url=https://variety.com/2020/film/news/oscars-2021-postponed-coronavirus-1234635457/ |access-date=September 19, 2020 |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=June 15, 2020 |archive-date=June 16, 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200616010655/https://variety.com/2020/film/news/oscars-2021-postponed-coronavirus-1234635457/ |url-status=live}} The annual Academy Governors Awards and corresponding nominees luncheon were canceled due to COVID-19 safety concerns.{{cite news |last1=Pond |first1=Steve |title=Academy Limits Oscars to Nominees and Their Guests, Cancels Governors Ball and Nominees Lunch |url=https://www.thewrap.com/academy-limits-oscars-to-nominees-and-their-guests-cancels-governors-ball-and-nominees-lunch/ |access-date=30 May 2021 |work=[[TheWrap]] |date=15 March 2021 |archive-date=June 3, 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210603065351/https://www.thewrap.com/academy-limits-oscars-to-nominees-and-their-guests-cancels-governors-ball-and-nominees-lunch/ |url-status=live}} This marked the first time since the [[60th Academy Awards|60th ceremony]] held in 1988 that the awards were held in April.{{cite news |last1=Finn |first1=Natalie |title=The 7 Biggest Jaw-Droppers at the 2021 Oscars |url=https://www.eonline.com/news/1262414/the-7-biggest-jaw-droppers-at-the-2021-oscars |access-date=June 17, 2021 |agency=[[E!]] |date=April 26, 2021 |archive-date=June 24, 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624201824/https://www.eonline.com/news/1262414/the-7-biggest-jaw-droppers-at-the-2021-oscars |url-status=live}} It also was the first time since the [[53rd Academy Awards|53rd ceremony]] in 1981 that the ceremony was postponed from its original date.{{cite news |last1=Martinez |first1=Peter |title=Next Year's Oscars Delayed to April 25 Due to the Coronavirus Pandemic |url=https://www.cbsnews.com/news/oscars-2021-delayed-coronavirus-pandemic/ |access-date=August 9, 2020 |agency=[[CBS News]] |date=June 15, 2020 |archive-date=August 8, 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200808153439/https://www.cbsnews.com/news/oscars-2021-delayed-coronavirus-pandemic/ |url-status=live}} In December 2020, the Academy hired television producer Jesse Collins, film producer [[Stacey Sher]], and Oscar-winning director [[Steven Soderbergh]] to oversee production of the telecast. "The upcoming Oscars is the perfect occasion for innovation and for re-envisioning the possibilities for the awards show. This is a dream team who will respond directly to these times. The Academy is excited to work with them to deliver an event that reflects the worldwide love of movies and how they connect us and entertain us when we need them the most," remarked Academy president David Rubin and CEO [[Dawn Hudson]].{{cite news |last1=Hammond |first1=Pete |title=Oscars: Steven Soderbergh, Stacey Sher & Jesse Collins To Produce 93rd Academy Awards |url=https://deadline.com/2020/12/oscars-steven-soderbergh-stacey-sher-jesse-collins-producers-academ-awardsto-produce-93rd-academy-awards-1234652331/ |access-date=December 30, 2020 |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |date=December 8, 2020 |archive-date=December 28, 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201228033747/https://deadline.com/2020/12/oscars-steven-soderbergh-stacey-sher-jesse-collins-producers-academ-awardsto-produce-93rd-academy-awards-1234652331/ |url-status=live}} The tagline for the ceremony, "Bring Your Movie Love", was intended to reflect "our global appreciation for the power of film to foster connection, to educate, and to inspire us to tell our own stories."{{cite news |last1=Hipes |first1=Patrick |title=Oscars Poster Revealed With This Year's Tagline "Bring Your Movie Love"; No Clues Offered About Ceremony |url=https://deadline.com/2021/03/oscars-2021-poster-key-art-revealed-1234712398/ |access-date=June 19, 2021 |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |date=March 11, 2021 |archive-date=June 24, 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624200758/https://deadline.com/2021/03/oscars-2021-poster-key-art-revealed-1234712398/ |url-status=live}} In tandem with the theme, the Academy hired AMPAS president Rubin and CEO Hudson explained the decision to extend the eligibility period saying, "For over a century, movies have played an important role in comforting, inspiring, and entertaining us during the darkest of times. They certainly have this year. Our hope, in extending the eligibility period and our Awards date, is to provide the flexibility filmmakers need to finish and release their films without being penalized for something beyond anyone's control."{{cite news |last1=Barnes |first1=Brooks |title=The 2021 Oscars Will Be Delayed |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2020/06/15/business/media/2021-oscars-delayed-coronavirus.html |access-date=August 13, 2020 |work=[[The New York Times]] |date=June 15, 2020 |url-access=limited |archive-date=August 21, 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200821192505/https://www.nytimes.com/2020/06/15/business/media/2021-oscars-delayed-coronavirus.html |url-status=live}} The Academy also revised its release and distribution requirements by allowing for films that were released via [[video on demand]] or [[streaming]] to be eligible for the awards on the condition that said films were originally scheduled to have a theatrical release and were subsequently uploaded to AMPAS's online screening service within 60 days of their public release. AMPAS also amended its theatrical exhibition qualifying rules to allow films debuting in theaters located in New York City, Chicago, the San Francisco Bay Area, Atlanta, and Miami to qualify for the awards in addition to venues in Los Angeles.{{cite news |last1=Feinberg |first1=Scott |title=Oscars During Coronavirus: Academy Rules Streamed Films Eligible, Merges Sound Categories |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/news/general-news/oscars-coronavirus-academy-rules-streamed-films-eligible-merges-sound-categories-1292043/ |access-date=June 19, 2020 |work=[[The Hollywood Reporter]] |date=April 28, 2020 |archive-date=June 24, 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624201256/https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/news/general-news/oscars-coronavirus-academy-rules-streamed-films-eligible-merges-sound-categories-1292043/ |url-status=live}} Moreover, a week of nightly screenings at a [[drive-in theater]] within the aforementioned cities also rendered films eligible for consideration.{{cite news |last1=Mandell |first1=Andrea |last2=Alexander |first2=Bryan |title=Academy Makes Major Change: Drive-in Movies Can Now Qualify for 2021 Oscars |url=https://www.usatoday.com/story/entertainment/movies/2020/10/07/oscars-academy-says-drive-movies-qualify-2021-ceremony/5917312002/ |access-date=November 17, 2020 |work=[[USA Today]] |date=October 7, 2020 |archive-date=November 6, 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201106132251/https://www.usatoday.com/story/entertainment/movies/2020/10/07/oscars-academy-says-drive-movies-qualify-2021-ceremony/5917312002/ |url-status=live}} Furthermore, the Academy made changes to specific award categories. The [[Best Sound Mixing]] and [[Academy Award for Best Sound Editing|Best Sound Editing]] categories were re-combined into a single Best Sound category (which had existed from the [[3rd Academy Awards|3rd Awards]] in 1930 through the [[75th Academy Awards|75th Awards]] in 2003) due to concerns from the Sound branch that the two categories had developed too much overlap in scope.{{cite news |last1=Ordoña |first1=Michael |title=Just One Oscar Category for Sound? The Pros Are Happy to Share the Acclaim |url=https://www.latimes.com/entertainment-arts/movies/story/2020-04-28/just-one-sound-category-the-pros-are-delighted-since-the-two-jobs-lines-have-blurred |url-status=live}} The rules for [[Academy Award for Best Original Score|Best Original Score]] were changed to require that a film's score include a minimum of 60% original music, with franchise films |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200523043653/https://deadline.com/2020/04/oscars-major-changes-academy-awards-coronavirus-1202919950/ |url-status=live}} === Best Actor announcement ending === [[File:Chadwick Boseman by Gage Skidmore July 2017 (cropped).jpg|thumb|right|alt=Chadwick Boseman in 2017.|Many viewers and television critics believed [[Chadwick Boseman]]'s impending win for Best Actor was a reason that producers saved the category as the last to be presented at the ceremony.]] In a break with tradition, the lead acting categories were presented last after the awarding of Best Picture, with Best Actor coming last.{{cite news |last1=Vary |first1=Adam B. |title=Oscars End Abruptly with Anthony Hopkins Best Actor Upset |url=https://variety.com/2021/awards/news/anthony-hopkins-best-actor-oscar-upset-1234959766/ |access-date=April 30, 2021 |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=April 25, 2021 |archive-date=April 26, 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210426162709/https://variety.com/2021/awards/news/anthony-hopkins-best-actor-oscar-upset-1234959766/ |url-status=live}} This led many viewers to believe that the ceremony's producers were anticipating Chadwick Boseman posthumously winning Best Actor, which could have been accompanied by a tribute to the actor; Boseman had been considered a strong frontrunner for the award.{{cite news |last1=Jackson |first1=Angelique |title=Anthony Hopkins Pays Tribute to Chadwick Boseman After Surprise Second Oscar Win |url=https://variety.com/2021/awards/news/anthony-hopkins-oscar-win-reaction-1234960502/ |access-date=July 2, 2021 |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=April 26, 2021 |archive-date=July 10, 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210710075831/https://variety.com/2021/awards/news/anthony-hopkins-oscar-win-reaction-1234960502/ |url-status=live}}{{cite news |last1=Buchanan |first1=Kyle |title=Inside Anthony Hopkins's Unexpected Win at the Oscars |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2021/04/26/movies/the-father-anthony-hopkins-oscars.html |access-date=May 23, 2021 |work=[[The New York Times]] |date=April 26, 2021 |url-access=limited |archive-date=May 15, 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210515101153/https://www.nytimes.com/2021/04/26/movies/the-father-anthony-hopkins-oscars.html |url-status=live}} When presenter [[Joaquin Phoenix]] announced that Anthony Hopkins was the winner of the category, Phoenix said that the Academy accepted the award on behalf of the latter, who was not present, and the ceremony came to an abrupt end. It was later reported that Hopkins, who did not want to travel from his home in Wales, offered to appear via [[Zoom (software)|Zoom]], but the producers declined his request. The day after the ceremony, he released an acceptance speech on Instagram, in which he thanked the Academy, said that he "really did not expect" to win, and paid tribute to Boseman. The selection of Hopkins over Boseman was controversial among viewers,{{Cite web |last1=Drury |first1=Sharareh |date=2021-04-26 |title=Oscars Face Backlash Over Chadwick Boseman Snub |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/oscars-criticized-chadwick-boseman-snub-4173199/ |access-date=2022-04-02 |website=The Hollywood Reporter |language=en-US |archive-date=April 2, 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220402085207/https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/oscars-criticized-chadwick-boseman-snub-4173199/ |url-status=live}}{{Cite web |last=Romero |first=Ariana |title=Chadwick Boseman Loses Best Actor In Shocking Oscars Finale |url=https://www.refinery29.com/en-us/2021/04/10443095/chadwick-boseman-oscar-best-actor-loss-twitter-reactions |access-date=2022-04-02 |website=www.refinery29.com |language=en |archive-date=April 2, 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220402085055/https://www.refinery29.com/en-us/2021/04/10443095/chadwick-boseman-oscar-best-actor-loss-twitter-reactions |url-status=live}} though Boseman's brother reported the family did not have any hard feelings toward the Academy.{{Cite web |last1=Sharf |first1=Zack |date=2021-04-27 |title=Chadwick Boseman's Family Speaks Out on Oscars Loss: This Was Not a Snub |url=https://www.indiewire.com/2021/04/chadwick-boseman-family-oscars-snub-1234633534/ |access-date=2022-04-02 |website=IndieWire |language=en |archive-date=April 2, 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220402085055/https://www.indiewire.com/2021/04/chadwick-boseman-family-oscars-snub-1234633534/ |url-status=live}} In a subsequent interview with the ''[[Los Angeles Times]]'', Soderbergh said that switching the traditional order of awards was planned before the nominations were announced, claiming "actors' speeches tend to be more dramatic than producers' speeches". He said that the possibility of Boseman's widow accepting the award "would have been such a shattering moment" and "there would be nowhere to go after that". Soderbergh also defended the decision to not allow acceptance speeches via Zoom.{{cite news |date=May 4, 2021 |title=That off-key Oscars ending? Had to be done, says producer Steven Soderbergh |url=https://www.latimes.com/entertainment-arts/movies/story/2021-05-04/steven-soderbergh-oscars-ending-chadwick-boseman-anthony-hopkins |access-date=May 13, 2021 |work=[[Los Angeles Times]] |language=en-US |url-access=limited |archive-date=May 13, 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210513012655/https://www.latimes.com/entertainment-arts/movies/story/2021-05-04/steven-soderbergh-oscars-ending-chadwick-boseman-anthony-hopkins |url-status=live}} ===Critical reviews=== Many media outlets received the broadcast negatively. {{Rotten Tomatoes prose|24|3=34|4=The 93rd Oscars definitely delivered something different, but after a strong opening moment with Regina King, the changes to this year's ceremony cemented the importance of certain structural traditions -- and how damaging hedging your bets on the Academy's votes can be.|ref=y|access-date=March 15, 2024}}{{Cite web |title=The Academy Awards: 93rd Oscars |url=https://www.rottentomatoes.com/tv/the_academy_awards/s93 |access-date=2024-03-15 |website=[[Rotten Tomatoes]] |publisher=[[Fandango Media]] |language=en |archive-date=April 26, 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210426061053/https://www.rottentomatoes.com/tv/the_academy_awards/s93 |url-status=live }} Television critic Mike Hale of ''[[The New York Times]]'' wrote, "Sunday's broadcast on ABC was more like a cross between the [[Golden Globes]] and the closing-night banquet of a long, exhausting convention." He also commented, "The trade-off — whether because of the smaller crowd, the social distancing, or the sound quality in the cavernous space — was what felt like a dead room, both acoustically and emotionally. There were powerful and moving speeches, but they didn't seem to be generating much excitement, and when the people in the room aren't excited, it's hard to get excited at home."{{cite news |last1=Hale |first1=Mike |title=The Covid-19 Oscars: Not Masked but Still Muffled |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2021/04/25/arts/television/covid-19-oscars-2021.html |access-date=May 9, 2021 |work=[[The New York Times]] |date=April 25, 2021 |url-access=limited |archive-date=May 9, 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210509134351/https://www.nytimes.com/2021/04/25/arts/television/covid-19-oscars-2021.html |url-status=live}} ''[[Rolling Stone]]'' columnist Rob Sheffield noted, "The most flamboyantly unplanned and half-assed Oscar Night in recent history was a grind from beginning to end." He also criticized the production of the "In Memoriam" segment saying that the montage was edited at an inappropriately fast pace.{{cite magazine |last1=Sheffield |first1=Rob |title=Oscars 2021: Wolf Howls, Da Butt and the Chadwick Boseman Tribute That Wasn't |url=https://www.rollingstone.com/movies/movie-features/oscars-2021-da-butt-rob-sheffield-1161148/ |access-date=May 17, 2021 |magazine=[[Rolling Stone]] |date=April 25, 2021 |url-access=limited |archive-date=May 4, 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210504200824/https://www.rollingstone.com/movies/movie-features/oscars-2021-da-butt-rob-sheffield-1161148/ |url-status=live}} Kelly Lawler of ''[[USA Today]]'' commented, "While it was certainly challenging to stage the show safely, [[63rd Annual Grammy Awards|last month's Grammys]] proved that it is possible to make something entertaining and engaging amid the pandemic. Unfortunately, the Oscars producers seemingly missed that show. The Oscars were a train wreck at the train station, an excruciatingly long, boring telecast that lacked the verve of so many movies we love."{{cite news |last1=Lawler |first1=Kelly |title=How the First (and Hopefully Last) Pandemic Oscars Went So Terribly Wrong |url=https://www.usatoday.com/in-depth/entertainment/tv/2021/04/26/oscars-2021-review-how-pandemic-ceremony-went-so-terribly-wrong/7292897002/ |access-date=May 23, 2021 |work=[[USA Today]] |date=April 26, 2021 |url-access=limited |archive-date=May 31, 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210531090925/https://www.usatoday.com/in-depth/entertainment/tv/2021/04/26/oscars-2021-review-how-pandemic-ceremony-went-so-terribly-wrong/7292897002/ |url-status=live}} Others gave a more favorable review of the show. ''[[Time (magazine)|Time]]'' columnist Judy Berman wrote that the ceremony "was more entertaining than the average pre-COVID Oscars. It started out especially strong." She also added, "Every part of this year's ceremony felt more intimate and less stuffy than just about any awards show I can remember. For once, the art and community of film seemed to take precedence over the business of film."{{cite magazine |last1=Berman |first1=Judy |title=The Pandemic Oscars Were Surprisingly Decent. But Will the Academy Learn Anything From Its Break With Tradition? |url=https://time.com/5958414/oscars-review-2021/ |access-date=May 31, 2021 |magazine=[[Time (magazine)|Time]] |date=April 26, 2021 |archive-date=April 29, 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210429165333/https://time.com/5958414/oscars-review-2021/ |url-status=live}} [[Associated Press]] reporter Lindsey Bahr commented, "The 93rd Academy Awards wasn't exactly a movie, but it was a show made for people who love learning about movies. And it stubbornly, defiantly wasn't trying to be anything else."{{cite news |last1=Bahr |first1=Lindsey |title=Review: Not Quite a Movie, but the Oscars Were a Love Letter |url=https://apnews.com/article/oscars-2021-review-c02d5d74c3615c74b81aa82af3df570f |access-date=May 31, 2021 |work=[[Associated Press]] |date=April 25, 2021 |archive-date=June 2, 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210602214146/https://apnews.com/article/oscars-2021-review-c02d5d74c3615c74b81aa82af3df570f |url-status=live}} Darren Franich of ''[[Entertainment Weekly]]'' gave an average review of the telecast, but singled out the winners and presenters for providing memorable moments throughout the show.{{cite magazine |last1=Franich |first1=Darren |title=What a Weird Oscars: Review |url=https://ew.com/awards/oscars/oscars-2021-review/ |access-date=May 25, 2021 |magazine=[[Entertainment Weekly]] |date=April 25, 2021 |archive-date=May 21, 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210521092711/https://ew.com/awards/oscars/oscars-2021-review/ |url-status=live}} ===Ratings and reception=== The American telecast on ABC drew in an average of 10.4 million people over its length, which was a 56% decrease from the [[92nd Academy Awards|previous year's ceremony]].{{cite news |last1=Richwine |first1=Lisa |last2=Grebler |first2=Dan |title=Final TV ratings for Oscars inch up to 10.4 million viewers |url=https://www.reuters.com/article/us-awards-oscars-ratings-idCAKBN2CE2D6 |access-date=June 19, 2021 |work=[[Reuters]] |date=April 27, 2021 |archive-date=June 24, 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624202839/https://www.reuters.com/article/us-awards-oscars-ratings-idCAKBN2CE2D6 |url-status=live}} The show also earned lower [[Nielsen ratings]] compared to the previous ceremony with 5.9% of households watching the ceremony. In addition, it garnered a lower rating among viewers between ages 18–49 with a 2.1 rating among viewers in that demographic.{{cite news |last1=Whitten |first1=Sarah |title=Audiences for Award Shows are in Steep Decline; This Chart Shows How Far Viewership Has Fallen |url=https://www.cnbc.com/2021/05/02/oscars-2021-nielsen-data-shows-viewers-have-lost-interest-in-award-shows.html |access-date=May lowest viewership for an Academy Award telecast since figures were compiled beginning with the [[46th Academy Awards|46th ceremony]] in 1974.{{cite news |last1=Smith |first1=Gerry |title=Oscars 12, 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210912000458/https://deadline.com/2021/09/creative-arts-emmy-awards-2021-winners-list-1234831219/ |url-status=live}} === Censorship in China and Hong Kong=== The ceremony was subject to various forms of censorship in China and its territories. Due to scrutiny over ''Nomadland'' director [[Chloé Zhao]]—a Chinese-American citizen who reportedly made comments critical of China in a 2013 interview with ''[[Filmmaker (magazine)|Filmmaker]]'' magazine—the ceremony telecast was pulled by its local rightsholders in the mainland, and all discussions of the ceremony were [[Censorship in China|censored from Chinese social media and news outlets]].{{cite news |date=2021-04-26 |title=Chloé Zhao's historic win at this year's Oscars censored in China |url=https://www.scmp.com/lifestyle/entertainment/article/3131131/nomadland-director-chloe-zhaos-historic-oscars-win-censored |access-date=2021-04-27 |work=[[South China Morning Post]] |language=en |archive-date=October 7, 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211007070954/https://www.scmp.com/lifestyle/entertainment/article/3131131/nomadland-director-chloe-zhaos-historic-oscars-win-censored |url-status=live}}{{cite news |last=Lin |first=Liza |date=2021-04-26 |title=China Censors 'Nomadland' Director Chloé Zhao's Oscar Win |language=en-US |work=[[The Wall Street Journal]] |url=https://www.wsj.com/articles/china-censors-nomadland-director-chloe-zhaos-oscar-win-11619440550 |access-date=2021-04-26 |issn=0099-9660 |archive-date=April 29, 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210429222410/https://www.wsj.com/articles/china-censors-nomadland-director-chloe-zhaos-oscar-win-11619440550 |url-status=live}} In addition, Hong Kong broadcaster [[TVB]] announced that the ceremony would not be shown live in the region for the first time since 1969. A TVB spokesperson told [[Agence France-Presse|AFP]] that this was a "commercial decision". It was speculated that the decision was in retaliation for the nomination of ''[[Do Not Split]]'', a documentary on [[2019–2020 Hong Kong protests|Hong Kong's pro-democracy protests in 2019]], for Best Documentary Short Subject.{{cite news |date=April 25, 2021 |title=Oscars won't show in Hong Kong for first time since 1969 as state media rails against protest film nomination
